term: yaṃ aniccaṃ taṃ dukkhaṃ; yaṃ dukkhaṃ tad anattā
literal_meaning: what is impermanent, that is dukkha; what is dukkha, that is not-self
preferred_translation: what is impermanent is dissatisfaction; what is dissatisfaction is not-self
alternative_translations: what is impermanent is unsatisfactory; what is unsatisfactory is not-self
discouraged_translations: what is impermanent suffers; what suffers has no self
definition: Compact progression formula linking impermanence, dissatisfaction, and not-self in one line.
notes: Use this record for the full progression line when the three marks are being coordinated explicitly. SN 22.15 anchors the line itself. It keeps the repository's house defaults for impermanent, dissatisfaction, and not-self audible in one sentence, and the OSF glossary supports keeping those defaults coordinated instead of switching ad hoc to suffering or no-self slogans. This is the cluster's most compact translator-facing map of how the three marks hang together.

context_rules.rendering: what is impermanent is dissatisfaction; what is dissatisfaction is not-self

context_rules.context: When surrounding prose already requires adjective-grade English for dukkha
context_rules.rendering: what is impermanent is unsatisfactory; what is unsatisfactory is not-self
context_rules.notes: Controlled alternate when the sentence grammar demands it.

example_phrases.pali: yaṃ aniccaṃ taṃ dukkhaṃ; yaṃ dukkhaṃ tad anattā
example_phrases.translation: what is impermanent is dissatisfaction; what is dissatisfaction is not-self
example_phrases.notes: Shows the compact progression line linking the full three-marks family.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the compact progression line itself.
Supports keeping the repository's house defaults coordinated across the three-marks line.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use as the standard rendering when the full impermanent -> dissatisfaction -> not-self progression is explicit.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not substitute suffering or no-self by habit, and do not break the line into uncoordinated term-level paraphrases when the formula itself is the point.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the compact three-marks progression from fragmenting into unrelated translation choices.
